[size=4][b]FloatingDamageNG[/b][/size]

[size=3][color=#D4D4D8]Floating damage numbers for Skyrim — one DLL for [b]SE, AE, and VR[/b], with true world-anchored 3D numbers in VR and a built-in combat analytics log. Fully open source.[/color][/size]

[size=4][b]What you see[/b][/size]
[color=#D4D4D8]
[list]
[*][b]Numbers colored by cause:[/b] physical, fire, frost, shock, poison, untyped magic, and green +N healing. An enchanted-weapon swing shows two numbers — the physical hit and the enchant payload — because the engine applies them separately and so do we.
[*][b]Real hit detail:[/b] CRIT / SNEAK / BLOCK from the engine's actual hit flags, and a mitigation subtext that says what happened: 104 (-45 armor), (-30 blocked), (-20 resisted) — sized by how much was mitigated. A fully immune target shows RESISTED in the element's color instead of nothing.
[*][b]HEADSHOT tags[/b] on projectile hits, read from the engine's impact skeleton node. Works in vanilla; stays accurate under locational-damage mods (an implied x2.5 shows when a hit exceeds its normal baseline).
[*][b]Numbers follow their target:[/b] a fleeing enemy under Flames carries its counting-up burn total; your self-heal rides along as you move. DoT ticks pool into one live number instead of spam.
[*][b]Readable in crowds:[/b] follower numbers smaller/dimmer, NPC-vs-NPC dimmer still and distance-culled. Three motion profiles (Float / Arc / Radial), damage-scaled sizing, real TTF fonts.
[*][b]Combat analytics:[/b] every fight logged as a session — per-combatant damage, healing, crits, time-to-die, who fled, real vs active DPS — to a plain-text log, with an optional in-game stats browser (via SKSE Menu Framework) and an optional live DPS readout (off by default).
[/list][/color]

[size=4][b]VR[/b][/size]
[color=#D4D4D8]In VR the numbers are real 3D billboards at the target's position, rendered through [url=https://github.com/alandtse/imgui-vr-helper]ImGuiVRHelper[/url]'s world-quad API with per-pixel depth occlusion — not a flat overlay on the HUD plane, which causes stereo convergence mismatch in a headset. This is currently the only floating-damage mod that renders in world space in VR.[/color]

[size=4][b]How it differs from Floating Damage / Modern Floating Damage[/b][/size]
[color=#D4D4D8]
[list]
[*][b]Damage data:[/b] this mod hooks the engine's damage and magic-effect pipelines, so amounts are true per-application values with source and caster. The original Floating Damage polls health deltas per frame and has to estimate regen away, with no source information. Modern Floating Damage also hooks the pipeline but renders through PrismaUI (an embedded web view) and is flat-screen only.
[*][b]VR:[/b] neither of the others supports VR.
[*][b]Open source:[/b] this is the only floating-damage mod that is open source in its current form (GPL-3.0 with a modding exception). Floating Damage only ever published an early version of its source and its author is no longer active; Modern Floating Damage is closed. In practice that means anyone can rebuild this mod when a game update lands, audit its hooks for compatibility, or fork it if it is ever abandoned.
[/list][/color]

[size=4][b]Requirements[/b][/size]
[color=#D4D4D8]SE/AE: SKSE64 + Address Library. VR: SKSE VR + VR Address Library + [url=https://github.com/alandtse/imgui-vr-helper]ImGuiVRHelper[/url] 1.5+. Optional: [url=https://github.com/alandtse/SKSE-Menu-Framework-3]SKSE Menu Framework[/url] for the in-game settings panel and stats browser. Install with MO2/Vortex; configure via [i]Data/SKSE/Plugins/FloatingDamageNG.ini[/i] (fully commented) or in-game.[/color]

[size=4][b]Source, bugs, contributions[/b][/size]
[color=#D4D4D8]Source and issue tracker on the [url=https://github.com/alandtse/FloatingDamageNG]GitHub repository[/url]. Bug reports with your FloatingDamageNG.log (and crash log if applicable) are the most useful thing you can send.[/color]
